factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has significantly impacted economic activity and markets around the world, and it could have a material negative impact on our business and operations in numerous ways, including but not limited to those outlined below.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: There is no specific mention of backlog orders or book-to-bill trends in the given text.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in maintaining pricing power due to lower demand and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: Higher water heater volumes were partially offset by a water heater sales mix composed of more electric models which have a lower selling price and lower contractual formula pricing associated with a portion of water heater sales based on lower steel costs.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin products, negatively impacting margins.

evidence.text: The unfavorable impact from lower China sales and a higher mix of mid-price products, which have lower margins compared to higher priced products, was partially offset by the benefits from lower SG&A expenses in that region.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, particularly in the Rest of World segment.

evidence.text: Sales in the first quarter of 2020 were $637 million, approximately 15% lower than sales of $748 million in the first quarter of 2019. Our lower sales in the first quarter of 2020 compared to the same period last year were primarily due to a 57% decline in sales in China, which was primarily driven by COVID-19 pandemic–related weak consumer demand.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, particularly in China, due to reduced consumer demand.

evidence.text: Sales in China decreased 57% in U.S. dollar terms and 56% in local currency terms, primarily due to COVID-19 pandemic related weak consumer demand as the Chinese economy was essentially closed and mobility was restricted for the majority of the first quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: End markets are weakening, particularly in China, while North America shows some resilience with growth in water treatment products.

evidence.text: North America Sales in our North America segment were $533 million in the first quarter of 2020, $11 million higher than sales of $522 million in the first quarter of 2019. The higher sales in the first quarter of 2020 were primarily due to incremental sales of $16 million from Water-Right, as well as organic growth of approximately 17% in our North America water treatment products.

factors.summary: Input costs are not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: There is no specific mention of raw material, commodity, or component cost trends in the given text.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through reductions and efficiency measures, but there is no mention of wage inflation or benefit expenses creating pressure.

evidence.text: We also continue to focus on right-sizing our cost structure in China through headcount reductions, store closures, cuts in advertising and other cost saving measures.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to lower sales volumes and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: First quarter gross profit margin of 37.6% in 2020 declined from a gross profit margin of 39.1% in the first quarter of 2019. The lower gross profit margin was primarily due to lower sales volumes in China.

factors.summary: Cost reduction measures, such as headcount reductions and store closures in China, are being implemented and are expected to deliver savings.

evidence.text: We also continue to focus on right-sizing our cost structure in China through headcount reductions, store closures, cuts in advertising and other cost saving measures.

factors.summary: Productivity is being affected by operational changes to maintain social distancing and safety measures, potentially reducing efficiency.

evidence.text: These important steps, which in certain cases reduce efficiency, include making plant accommodations and reconfigurations to maintain social distancing, mask availability to all employees, during deep cleanings of our facilities and encouraging employees to work remotely where possible, among others.

factors.summary: The supply chain is experiencing disruptions and is under close monitoring, but continues to operate without meaningful disruptions so far.

evidence.text: Our global supply chain management team continues to monitor and manage our ability to operate effectively in response to various and differing shelter-in-place orders by countries and states where we and our suppliers operate. To date, we have not seen any meaningful disruptions to our supply chain.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is at below-normal levels, particularly in China, due to the pandemic.

evidence.text: As of the date of this filing, our operations in China have resumed but at below normal levels.

factors.summary: Inventory levels in China are declining slightly, but overall inventory levels are not explicitly addressed.

evidence.text: China channel inventory levels declined slightly in the first quarter of 2020 from the levels at the end of 2019.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety incidents are mentioned in the provided text.

evidence.text: There is no specific mention of product quality, safety incidents, or warranty costs in the given text.

factors.summary: Management has suspended forward guidance for 2020 due to the uncertainty caused by the pandemic.

evidence.text: As a result we suspended our 2020 full year outlook. As the depth and duration of the disruption and pace of recovery in our end markets become clearer, we will look to return to our practice of providing a current year outlook.

factors.summary: Management is showing caution by suspending guidance, implementing cost reductions, and reducing executive compensation.

evidence.text: The members of our Board of Directors have voluntarily reduced the cash component of their board compensation by 25% and our chairman and chief executive officer (CEO) has voluntarily reduced his base salary by 25%. Our CEO’s staff, which includes our other named executive officers, have also volunteered a 15% reduction in base salary.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing disruptions from the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly in China and other global markets, and potential economic downturns.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has significantly impacted economic activity and markets around the world, and it could have a material negative impact on our business and operations in numerous ways, including but not limited to those outlined below:

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is to focus on essential products and markets, and it continues to invest in new products and technology.

evidence.text: We continue to invest strategically in our business focusing on productivity, new products and technology.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to economic conditions, particularly in China and other global markets, and the impact of the pandemic on consumer spending.

evidence.text: While we expect that our replacement business in both water heating and boilers will provide a buffer in any economic downturn resulting from COVID-19 in a similar manner to what we have seen historically, the impacts of the pandemic on consumer spending are difficult to predict.

factors.summary: The resumption of normal business operations post-pandemic is a potential catalyst for improved results.

evidence.text: As the severity and duration of the disruption and pace of recovery in our end markets become clearer, we will look to return to our practice of providing a current year outlook.
